title: phaseolus polystachios subsp. smilacifolius
intro: Phaseolus polystachios subsp. smilacifolius, also known as wild kidney or prairie bean, is a subspecies of the prairie bean plant. It is a perennial legume native to the Great Plains region of North America, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phaseolus polystachios subsp. smilacifolius include its potential to reduce blood sugar levels, lower cholesterol, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its ability to protect against certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be used to make medicinal remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
